items.title: Specialized shuttle proteins recognize Type IX secretion signals and target effectors to their final destinations in Flavobacterium johnsoniae
items.author: Thierry Doan
items.content: Bacteroidota use the Type IX secretion system to secrete proteins with a conserved C-terminal domain (CTD) secretion signal domain. Type B CTDs require specific shuttle proteins for secretion and target substrates to their final localization.

items.title: Deficiency of Shank3 in the nucleus accumbens reveals a loss of social-specific motivation
items.author: Yong-hui Jiang
items.content: Conditional removal of autism risk gene Shank3 from a reward encoding brain region reveals a unique mechanism for social motivation that is separate from naturalistic social interaction and non-social motivated behavior.

items.title: Structural basis for aerobic anoxygenic photosynthesis in the reaction center–light-harvesting 1 (RC–LH1) supercomplex of Dinoroseobacter shibae
items.author: Peng Wang
items.content: A cryo-EM structure for a marine bacterium’s photosynthetic complex reveals key adaptations that explain how it balances photosynthesis with oxidative resilience to thrive in oxygen-rich environments.

items.title: Parameter optimization for MINFLUX microscopy enabled single particle tracking
items.author: Francesco Reina
items.content: A Single Particle Tracking parameter optimization pipeline for MINFLUX microscopy.

items.title: Human brain R2* transitions across birth from the womb to early infancy
items.author: Moriah E. Thomason
items.content: Using 147 MRIs (25–60 weeks), this first longitudinal study of R2* across fetal to neonatal periods shows distinct regional trajectories, with linear and non-linear patterns reflecting iron-related maturation and perinatal adaptation.

items.title: Structures of CRP antigen-antibody complexes provide insights into the mechanism of specific recognition
items.author: Fuxing Zeng
items.content: Structural differences in CRP antigen-antibody engagement conformations elucidate distinct binding modalities, providing mechanistic insights for the rational optimization of CRP-specific antibodies.

items.title: Engineering a heparin-mimetic biomaterial to promote tissue vascularization
items.author: Christopher S. Chen
items.content: Synthetic heparin-mimetic hydrogels immobilize angiogenic growth factors to promote tissue vascularization, providing salient properties of heparin-containing materials in promoting angiogenesis without the associated risk of bleeding.

items.title: Ben_0691 functions as an IRG-47 mycoplasma stimulator that mediates host cell resistance to Mycoplasma mycoides subsp. mycoides infection
items.author: Qiao Pan
items.content: Mechanistic modeling of Ben_0691-induced IRG-47 release reveals a host cell resistance mechanism against Mmm infection, providing insights into protective immunity and guiding next-generation vaccine development.
